svg.text: Each fruit shows its variety and harvest date
Why this matters
The catalogue lumps every fruit into one generic "fruit" row,
so customers can't search by apple variety. Surfacing variety
and harvest date is the precondition for filter and notify.
Where things stand
Catalogue shows one row per orchard with fruit type as a single
string. No variety, no harvest date, no Cox vs Braeburn.
What we'll do
• Add Variety model with name, kind enum, and notes
• Add harvested_on date to the Fruit model
• Render variety + harvest date on each catalogue row
What we won't do
• No filter or search-by-variety UI yet — data only
• No editing varieties from the catalogue page
Expected outcome
• Each row shows variety name (or "—") and harvest date
• A customer tells Cox from Braeburn at a glance
How to test it
Write tests first. Unit: kind enum + past-date validation.
Integration: catalogue page renders both fields. Manual:
load /catalogue against staging seed data.
Assumptions to confirm
Legacy fruit_type maps cleanly to the kind enum — confirm
before running the backfill migration.
Mock-up. Story ships as one PR; the parent epic ticks 1/8 when this closes.
